ops.title: ccx vcs ship works from a jj secondary workspace but its CI watch (gh) fails there — no .git dir; watch from the primary checkout
ops.body: `jj workspace add` creates a non-colocated workspace (no `.git`), which is the right isolation move when another session is actively editing the default working copy (seen 2026-07-11: two live sessions on getaway). Inside it, `ccx vcs ship` commits and pushes fine, but the CI-watch leg reports `CI error` because `gh` needs a git dir. The push itself succeeded — do NOT re-run ship (it would cut a new commit); run `gh run list --commit <full-sha>` (full sha per [[gh-run-list-commit-full-sha]]) from the primary checkout and watch there. Also: `git diff` doesn't work in these workspaces — use `jj diff --git`; codex needs `--skip-git-repo-check`. Clean up with `jj workspace forget <name>` then remove the dir. Related: [[jj-concurrent-session-sibling-recovery]].
